title: Setting The Tone For The Rest Of The Journey | Episode 3 | Dr Tim Pearce VLOG
filename: Setting The Tone For The Rest Of The Journey ｜ Episode 3 ｜ Dr Tim Pearce VLOG.en.vtt
excerpt: Goodbye boys bye [Music] it's Saturday I'm doing a foundation course today I'm running a bit late because I'm trying to vlog at the same time as get ready which is really quite hard hopefully it'll be worth it in the end if I can create something fun and something useful for you …

segments.text: goodbye boys bye [Music] it's Saturday I'm doing a foundation course today I'm running a bit late because I'm trying to vlog at the same time as get ready which is really quite hard hopefully it'll be worth it in the end if I can create something fun and something useful for you guys to know [Music]

[Music] that's such a busy day all right I'm gonna batter using it through the day so everything about the angle that needle is to avoid the artery if you gone in like that you're heading up towards country meets nose so it's always that way parallel 90 degrees to the facial plane really and then 45

degrees in the other plane like that don't just enjoy on the pillow just like horizontal 2.6 b15 in okay just look at that little massage you know I yep it's basically I'm not gonna go again but I'm just thinking of landmark I'm in surgeon it's basically creating a V with the needle on the edge of the nose if I was to remember in simple terms yeah that makes sense so what would they be so if I was to insert the needle like this because what I was doing was like this mm-hmm that's tool to the artery so if I was to go like this then you can see that I've created a V with the edge of the nose I mean just for me to remember yeah exactly the position you want to do that [Laughter]

so that's another training they done I always think it's a massive responsibility that the first day that you trained someone or it's like the first day someone joins my company because it kind of sets the tone for the rest of that journey and the tone the first time I check I train was a little on the negative side he knows a little bit critical of people treated the models a little bit like basically a piece of meat going through that needed to be used to train people and I'm really pleased to say that's not how we do things here and hopefully given those guys a real sense of the real value that they can do to generate to when they treat people and how to do it safely and how to have that safe approach and the fact that they're now part of a network so they're gonna get really good support from people who are a little bit further along the process than them and but I love training is a big responsibility but it's really good fun as well so I hope that gives you a little bit of insight what it's like that was our foundation day training thanks for watching

watching time [Music] it's no old glamour I've lost my key fob so it's time to climb over this fence [Applause] Easy's forgot it I don't even see it with a sky looks amazing I'm have to get the drone out [Music]
